userNote: File cannot load. Top-level requires reference three non-existent or wrong paths: (a) `./sonicClient.js` should be `../search/sonic-client` after Batch 027; (b) `./multiPassAnalyzer.js` does not exist anywhere in the repo; (c) `./precisionCapture.js` does not exist anywhere in the repo. Even though we documented the module, the first `require()` of this file from any caller will throw MODULE_NOT_FOUND. Need founder decision: delete the broken require lines, comment them out and stub the classes, port multiPassAnalyzer + precisionCapture from MAESTRO, or rewrite to skip them and write directly into InsightStore (per PM-1 Layer 2 alternative path).
actionsTaken: Fix path (a): point to ../search/sonic-client.js. For (b) and (c) — MAESTRO assets never ported — replaced the broken requires with inert no-op stand-ins that log a single warning on first invocation (no silent fake success). Module now loads cleanly; PM-1 Layer 2 remains dormant and the founder's port-vs-rewrite decision in the roadmap still stands. Verified: `node -e "require('.../background-indexer')"` returns cleanly. Commit 4fea31f.

userNote: Line 22 requires `../../core/database/graph-persister.js`. Path resolves (the file exists at src/core/database/graph-persister.js and exports `getSharedDatabasePath` + `DatabasePersister`), so this is OK today — but worth a watcher: this trio was kebab-cased on import and any future rename of graph-persister would silently break Sonic indexing.
actionsTaken: Annotation only — no behavior change. Added a watcher comment above the require explaining that the trio (client/indexer/queries) all share this dependency and a future rename of graph-persister.js would silently break Sonic indexing without test coverage. Commit 07e3c4d.

userNote: SQLite fallback IS wired — every public method has a matching `*SQLite` private implementation (findImportsSQLite, findConsumersSQLite, searchSymbolsSQLite, fetchDirectorySubgraphSQLite, suggestFromSQLite, fetchRelatedFilesSQLite). The fallback path triggers when `trySonicQuery()` returns an empty array OR when Sonic throws. Confirmed: the code path exists and the return shape tags `source: 'sqlite' | 'sonic+sqlite'`. No fix needed; closing observation only — caller needs to actually inspect `source` to know which path served the query, and today no consumer does.
actionsTaken: Annotation only — no behavior change. Added a JSDoc block on the SonicQueries class documenting the fallback pattern, the public-to-SQLite method pairs, and the source-tag-but-no-consumer observation. Commit 8d4bce7.

userNote: No tests for the lifecycle. start/stop/restart/adopt-external/health-check-timeout paths are exercised only by the bible's smoke target (`node start.js /tmp/st8-smoke-target`). Need unit tests at minimum for: (a) binary_missing graceful degrade, (b) adopt-if-already-running (mock TCP listener on 1491), (c) health-check timeout → SIGTERM cleanup, (d) idempotent re-start. Lifecycle bugs here cascade into every search query.

Line 159: `fs.chmodSync(SONIC_BINARY, 0o755)` runs on every `start()`. The binary only needs the executable bit set once (post-clone or post-CI-extract). Re-chmoding is harmless but wasteful and could mask permission issues if someone tightens perms intentionally. Worth one-shotting (check first, only chmod if not executable) or moving to a post-install hook.
actionsTaken: Moved chmod to module-load via ensureBinaryExecutable(). Idempotent: stat-checks owner +x bit and only chmods if missing; sets a module-scoped flag so re-starts skip the syscall. start() still calls the helper as a safety net for late-materialized binaries. Verified binary stays -rwxr-xr-x. Commit 76f2dca.

userNote: Upstream Sonic v1.4.9 panics on broken-pipe — smoke tests caught the Rust daemon dying when a client disconnects uncleanly (mid-PUSH, lost socket, etc.). This is an upstream issue. Mitigation today is that sonic-daemon's adopt-if-already-running path would re-attach after a systemd restart, but inside st8 a panicked Sonic leaves the daemon in `available: false` until next `start()`. Worth either: (a) auto-restart on unexpected child exit, (b) wrapping the sonic-client's send/recv to retry on ECONNRESET, or (c) filing an upstream issue with a repro.

`APP_ID = 'com.scaffolder.app'` is the MAESTRO-era identifier. Founder decision needed: should st8 (a) keep `com.scaffolder.app` because the directory IS the maestro exchange-surface and they're meant to share it, (b) switch to `com.st8.app` and break the bridge (which doesn't exist yet anyway), or (c) make APP_ID configurable so st8-standalone deployments don't collide with a maestro install. This is the explicit deferred-by-founder call from the bible.

Sonic store is per-target (`<targetDir>/.st8/sonic-store/`). What happens on re-index? Sonic's FST consolidates periodically (`consolidate_after = 180` in sonic.cfg), but the RocksDB KV grows indefinitely until manually compacted — old `node_*`/`file_*` IDs that no longer exist in the graph are never popped. No GC path. Long-running projects will see the on-disk store accumulate. Decide: (a) full POP+repush on every index, (b) deletion of `.st8/sonic-store/` before each re-index, (c) leave it (Sonic is small enough — 21 MB / 1M objects — that even 10x growth is OK), (d) add a `--rebuild-sonic` CLI flag.

No CI / lint check that the canonical `docs/Sonic/sonic.cfg` stays in sync with what `sonic-daemon.js` expects. The daemon hardcodes: port 1491, IPv4 host, `auth_password` not read (relies on MAESTRO's `maestro_scaffolder_key`), `${SONIC_STORE_PATH}` substitution. If MAESTRO bumps the config (renames sections, changes port, removes the env-var substitution), the daemon will spawn Sonic with a config it can't query. Need either: (a) a parse-and-validate step in the daemon's start path, (b) a CI assertion that key fields exist, or (c) explicit version-pinning of the upstream sonic.cfg.
actionsTaken: Picked option (a) — added validateSonicConfig() to sonic-daemon.js that runs before spawn. Checks required sections, declared port matches SONIC_PORT, auth_password key present, ${SONIC_STORE_PATH} substitution appears at least twice (kv + fst). On failure, start() returns the specific reason and falls through to SQLite-only mode. 9 unit tests cover canonical-cfg pass + each synthesized failure mode. Commit 4154b85.

userNote: auth_password in sonic.cfg is `maestro_scaffolder_key` — hardcoded and shared. Anyone with localhost access can hit Sonic. For local-dev this is fine; for any deployment that exposes Sonic beyond loopback (which the daemon doesn't do today, but a future systemd unit might) this is a credential leak. Worth either: (a) generating a per-target random password and patching both sonic.cfg and the client config at materialize-time, (b) keeping the shared key but documenting the loopback-only contract explicitly, or (c) deferring until a deployment story exists.
actionsTaken: Picked option (a), mirroring Wave 2C .st8/server.secret pattern. New ensureSonicPassword(targetDir) writes .st8/sonic.password (mode 0600, 32 random bytes hex) on first start. Runtime cfg materialization replaces auth_password in the patched copy; sonic-daemon then calls sonicClient.setPassword() (new method) so the singleton's three channels use the new key on next connect. Canonical docs/Sonic/sonic.cfg untouched. Graceful degrade to canonical key on filesystem error. 7 unit tests cover file creation, idempotency, mode 0600, regenerate-on-truncation, setPassword across channels, invalid-input rejection, runtime-cfg patching. Commit 86218e1.

userNote: `stop()` uses a synchronous spin-wait (`while (Date.now() - start < SHUTDOWN_GRACE_MS && !child.killed)`) on lines 260-263. This burns 1.5 s of CPU in a tight loop on every shutdown and blocks the event loop. Should be either an async setTimeout + child.once('exit') race, or skip the grace window entirely (SIGTERM then SIGKILL immediately if still alive on next tick).
